title: OpenTracing First Impl
body:
Intentionally not squashed as I'm not sure if the PR should be broken up further.
There is also a relatively large corresponding PR to containers/image.

Nothing crazy going on, just a simple implementation of OpenTracing.

comments.body: Nice! I'll do a more thorough review later, but a once-over finds nothing wrong
/approve
/ok-to-test
the test failures look legit on PAPR. Is there an issue with vendoring of code?
@baude Ah you're right I didn't vendor anything in, thanks.
/retest
@sjug looks like a gofmt error..
gofmt errors.  Wonder if we're getting bit by the version diff of gofmt between go1.10 and go1.11.
Thanks, I missed that. Not sure what was the cause as I'm on 1.11+ everywhere.
What do I need for `does not have a valid DCO`?

All of you PR's need to be signed
git commit -a --amend -s

I agree @giuseppe, should I separate `vendor/github.com/urfave/cli/context.go` into a different commit or a different PR altogether?

> should I separate `vendor/github.com/urfave/cli/context.go` into a different commit or a different PR altogether?

Thanks, a different patch is fine for me

@sjug Sadly needs a rebase.
Thanks @rhatdan, I've also split the library change into its own commit.
@giuseppe I've added the check, is that satisfactory?
/test images
/retest
/retest

I don't see an open pull request for `urfave/cli`. Downstream patches are likely to cause issues for some users and packagers, so I am having mixed feelings about that. `urfave/cli` is a bit abandoned but some maintainers respond when being pinged directly on a PR or issue.

Both commits are missing a description. It's really important to add some context to changes as the Github history might be lost at some point. It also helps to check the authors' intention against the proposed changes and during maintenance (e.g., bisecting and chasing bugs).

@vrothberg Thanks for the feedback as I see you have experience getting PRs into `urfave/cli`, I opened https://github.com/urfave/cli/pull/779 with the change. Who specifically should I ping? AudriusButkevicius? What is the best way to reach out to the maintainers, just mention them in the PR or reach out directly via email?

I had longer commit messages originally, but there was no `\n` between the "title" and the "description" so it was failing the CI checks for length. I will add some back.

> Who specifically should I ping? AudriusButkevicius? What is the best way to reach out to the maintainers, just mention them in the PR or reach out directly via email?

I pinged the maintainers who responded last time directly in your PR. Thanks a lot for opening it!

> I had longer commit messages originally, but there was no \n between the "title" and the "description" so it was failing the CI checks for length. I will add some back.

Thanks a lot :)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1715) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

With the help of @vrothberg I opened https://github.com/urfave/cli/pull/779, for the cli library change. The `urfave/cli` maintainers would like to keep the context out of the library, and provided some helpful suggestions for alternative solutions. I will update the PR pulling the library change from this PR and changing the implementation to suit.

/test images
@vrothberg WIP just pulling the `context.Context` field out of `cli.Context`. Will be failing for a bit this morning.
@sjug Are you still working on this?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1734) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@rhatdan As of the last update the `urfave/cli` maintainers thought I shouldn't use the library to carry the Context so I updated the PR to suit. So I reverted the library change and now it uses a (not ideal) global Context variable in main, at this point I need a code review. Will rebase when I get to the office.

The last commit "Unable to import Ctx to runtime package" fails validation for lack of DCO as I do not want to carry it the way it is now. I have commented out `GetRuntime()` as the global variable can't be read from that package. When we have some time to look at it, I'm open to suggestions on what I should do, or just drop that altogether?

@sjug I think we're open to moving `GetRuntime()` where it needs to be - there's no longer any reason for it not to be in the same package as everything else, which should allow you to hit the globals you need?
@mheon Yeah I think that's all I need in podman itself, the rest is in the libs

Always a good time here...

In the gating log:
```
cd /go/src/github.com/containers/libpod && ./hack/tree_status.sh
tree is dirty, please commit all changes and sync the vendor.conf
```

Meanwhile in real life:
```
[jugs@trip libpod]$ cd ~/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod && ./hack/tree_status.sh
tree is clean
```

LGTM
IDK what's up, but it looks like vendor isn't happy with this atm.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I can't reproduce whatever the gating failure is? As I mentioned [up here](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1692#issuecomment-462148114).
Any suggestions for how to fix this?
@vrothberg Mind taking a look at the vendoring failure in gating?
Code LGTM

> @vrothberg Mind taking a look at the vendoring failure in gating?

The scripts claims inconsistencies with the following files:
```
D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/LICENSE D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/README.md D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/ext/tags.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/globaltracer.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/gocontext.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/log/field.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/log/util.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/noop.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/propagation.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/span.go D vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/tracer.go M vendor/github.com/uber/jaeger-lib/metrics/factory.go M vendor/github.com/uber/jaeger-lib/metrics/metrics.go ?? vendor/github.com/uber/jaeger-lib/metrics/histogram.go
```

One issue is that github.com/uber/jaeger-lib is vendoring a branch, which is something we don't do/allow anymore for various reasons such as reproducibility, maintainability and also for packaging. Some distributions do not use the ./vendor folder but package all dependencies separately. Having a branch makes all these things very hard.

Another issue seems to be that too many files are added to vendor/github.com/opentracing/opentracing-go/. One way to resolve the issue is running `make vendor` locally and adding the remaining changes. After that, the script should pass.
@vrothberg Thank you, I have updated the opentracing deps to use commits instead and run `make vendor` as suggested.
Thanks for the assist @vrothberg !
A pleasure :) The script is fairly new. Such feedback is really helpful in improving documentation and the output of the script as well.
/lgtm

Thanks @rhatdan and all reviewers! This PR initializes the tracing spans in the top level functions (run, pull, etc), but then we also need the further [containers/image PR](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/524) to continue the trace into the libs as well.
